groups.explanation: The harness is correct: it uses H5Pset_file_image with the CORE VFD and H5Fopen, which is the documented pattern for in-memory HDF5 fuzzing. The OOM occurs in H5C__load_entry:1155 where H5MM_realloc is called with actual_len ≈ 4.29 GB. The EOA guard (H5C__verify_len_eoa) fails to catch this because the EOA itself is derived from the stored_eof field in the fuzz-controlled superblock; a crafted input sets both fields to near-UINT32_MAX, causing the EOA check to pass and the library to attempt an unbounded allocation on corrupt input.
groups.suggestedFixDirection: In H5C__load_entry (H5Centry.c ~line 1147-1155), add a hard upper-bound check on actual_len before calling H5MM_realloc — e.g., reject or clamp actual_len to a value no larger than the physical file/image size — so that a fuzz-controlled stored_eof cannot bypass the EOA guard and trigger an unbounded allocation.
